description_long: This object is a stylized tombstone, commonly found in graveyards or cemeteries. It features a tall, upright slab with a rounded top and angular shoulders, set on a stepped rectangular base. The front face is inscribed with large, hand-drawn letters spelling 'RIP', and the surface is marked with several cracks, giving it an aged and weathered appearance. The back is plain, lacking any inscriptions or decorative elements. The material appears to be a dark, matte stone, likely granite or concrete, with a rough, unpolished finish. The overall design is simple and iconic, evoking a classic grave marker often seen in urban parks or historical cemeteries.
description: A dark, weathered stone tombstone with 'RIP' inscribed on the front and a stepped rectangular base. The surface is cracked and aged, typical of classic graveyard markers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the 'RIP' inscription and visible surface cracks on the tombstone.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and stepped base, with no visible inscriptions.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, unadorned back with the same stepped base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and lack of detail on the sides.
